description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a fabric-covered fuselage and wings, supported by a framework of struts and wires. The wings are straight and stacked one above the other, with roundels painted on the upper surfaces, suggesting a military or historical design. The tail section features a distinctive H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and horizontal stabilizers, each adorned with colored markings. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, all fixed and exposed. The cockpit is open and positioned forward, just behind the propeller, with a single seat visible. The propeller is mounted at the nose, indicating a single piston engine in a tractor configuration.
description: A vintage biplane with fabric wings, open cockpit, and H-tail, featuring roundels and fixed landing gear. The structure is supported by struts and wires, with a nose-mounted propeller.
description_view_0: Rear three-quarter view showing the twin vertical stabilizers, H-tail, and the open cockpit behind the wings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the stacked biplane wings, roundels, nose-mounted propeller, and open cockpit.
description_view_2: Opposite rear three-quarter view, again emphasizing the H-tail and the structure of the wings and struts.
description_view_3: Direct rear view showing the full span of the wings, H-tail, and the exposed framework of the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with straight, parallel wings and no winglets; supported by interplane struts and wires.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the propeller.
